data.epi_content:
I opened my eyes and looked straight ahead to see Vivian sitting in a chair, drinking tea and reading a book.

Vivian glanced at me briefly and said indifferently.

"Nothing happened, right?"

"Yeah."

I pulled my knees up to my chest, buried my face in them, and replied.

"Nothing happened at all."

Vivian stood up, closed her book, and brought me a cup of tea and some cookies.

I quietly accepted and ate them.

They tasted good.

Which made it all the more miserable.

Even if they didn't taste good, even if it was turnip bread, I wanted to eat it somewhere I was free.

I wanted attention, but not the kind that comes from a vertical relationship.

I just wanted to be seen as an equal human being.

But to them, I don't seem to be a person.

In a way, to Evan, I'm probably just someone who helps maintain his relationship with Vivian, and to Vivian, I'm not sure, but I must be something she has special feelings for.

I don't know if everyone's first friend is so unusual.

When I think about Evan, my first friend, I wonder if it's different because he's of the opposite sex.

"If you're done, shall we go?"

"...Sure."

We both act as if absolutely nothing happened last night.

But while we can pretend nothing happened, we can't actually make it unhappen.

My heart is already in tatters, and whether I've left a small thorn or a dagger in Vivian and Evan's hearts, I've definitely left a mark.

"You're not going to let me leave here, are you?"

I say to Vivian as we walk down the hallway.

And she firmly answers, "Yes."

After that, even when Vivian tried to lighten the mood with conversation, I just nodded.

Tears would occasionally well up, but I'd quickly hide them with my sleeve before Vivian could see.

"Nothing happened, right?"

I ask the question again.

"Yes."

The returning answer was so cruel.

It was like telling me to never even think about leaving.

That sounded to me like I should never dream of living as a human being.

So I just felt the pain and swallowed it inside.

If it feels like what little heart I have left is rotting away, is that just my imagination?

I don't know.

......

Almost two weeks have passed with Lydia's very minor harassment—though it's quite painful, it feels insignificant compared to how Evan and Vivian keep me confined.

Two weeks, and as always, I eat and walk around with Evan and Vivian.

I didn't bother expressing my emotions.

I didn't bother opening my mouth.

Occasionally when tears threatened to fall, I'd let them slip out slightly and then hide them with my sleeve.

I was a good little bitch.

I didn't bark, didn't bite, didn't cause trouble.

I took care of my business, ate whatever food was thrown at me, crawled when told to crawl, and forgot when told to forget.

My master says nothing happened.

Then nothing happened.
data.epi_content2:
Another month passed.

Lydia uses wet cloths to torment—no, this is more like torture.

She would place several handkerchiefs on my face and pour water or tea from a teapot over them.

With amateur magic, she would dry my wet body and uniform, leaving no trace, making it a petty, wicked, and effective form of bullying.

I cried.

I cursed Lydia.

And what came back wasn't a slap on the cheek, but just a few towels placed on me with water poured over them.

Thanks to this, I can't drink tea these days.

I'm always drinking it through my nostrils, so just smelling the aroma makes me dizzy.

My two masters say they've started dating.

My male master said so while avoiding my gaze, and Vivian, no, my female master couldn't even meet my eyes, but how could a worthless dog like me object to my two masters forming a relationship?

I smiled brightly at them, as if sincerely blessing them, as if we were still friends like before, as if I had long since overcome any past pain, and offered them words of praise.

They began to feel at ease and tried to continue getting closer to me.

A dog that obeys well is welcomed everywhere.

Though it's a bit awkward to say it myself, I must be quite a cute dog.

While I may fall short compared to my female master, I did possess a much more beautiful appearance than others.

Once I gave up everything and started living as a dog, things finally became easier.

I clearly thought of myself as human when I wasn't.

Well, how could someone like me who killed several people be human?

So I was a dog.

I was thrown into this hell to be punished.

God must have commanded me to live not as a human but as a dog.

One day, as I was following my two masters around on their invitation-disguised-as-orders, eating and taking walks, the Crown Prince came looking for me.

As if asking my masters if he could borrow their dog, he told Evan he needed to discuss family matters with me and took me to a bench in front of a pond where carp lived.

Evan told the Crown Prince, bold as it was, to bring me back after our conversation.

He replied with a friendly smile that he would do so, but didn't hide his cold eyes.

Perhaps I was the only one who noticed.

After all, a dog is much more sensitive to energy than humans.

I look at the carp once, then at the wooden pillar nearby.

This was the place where I became a dog.

"Aren't you going to die?"

That was the first thing he said after bringing me here.

And while smoking an extremely strong cigar, at that.

"Give me one too, please."

"Of course, by the boundless grace of the Crown Prince."

Saying that, he handed me a cigar and lit it for me.

I took a drag and moved the smoke around in my mouth.

Perhaps because it had been so long since I'd smoked, my heart was beating extremely fast and I felt dizzy.

"It seems better to die as a human than to live as a dog."

"I've experienced dying as a human many times."

I say this with the same hope I once had—that if I died after leaving the Academy, this cycle might end, or if someone else killed me, this torment would be over.

With the same hope as back then, looking toward the future, haha. How ambitious.

"Well, there are humans who value life above all else.

Still, I've been observing you lately. And I've also looked into what kind of life you've lived since your family fell."

"You're the one who destroyed it, Your Highness."

Hearing my response, he looks surprised.

"Even knowing that, I have no intention of blaming you, resenting you, or begging for my life while wailing in front of you, so you can rest easy."

"I'm not sure if that's rational or foolish."

"It's neither—it's just meaningless.

What difference would it make if a person walked up to a piece of iron and said something, or brought a wooden stick and hit it?"

"Is there something you want? I could grant one or two things."
data.epi_content3:
"Is this pity?"

"Yes."

"One or two things granted directly by the Crown Prince—that's quite generous for an entire family."

"Indeed, usually nothing is given and more is taken away."

"It's impressive that you can say that out loud."

"So, what do you want?"

"A pistol and six bullets."

"I'm a bit afraid to hand that to a madwoman.

How about a rope?"

"I don't like pain."

"What about jumping from a building?"

"I just told you, I don't like pain.

And I don't want to die in an ugly way either. Unless there's no other option."

The truth is, I'm afraid of falling to my death from the sky.

I hate the idea of falling, enduring that speed while trembling in fear, and with my strangely good luck, I might survive as a cripple.

In that case, there would truly be no way out.

I probably wouldn't be able to die until old age took me.

After I said this, the Crown Prince pondered for a moment, tapping his chin.

Then, after taking a drag from his cigar and exhaling a cloud of smoke, he spoke.

"Do you resent those two who are keeping you confined?"

"Well, I don't particularly resent Your Highness who destroyed my family and killed my brother, so probably not."

When I mentioned my brother, he closed his mouth.

After all, it was the Imperial family who fabricated ridiculous charges, branded him a traitor, and killed him.

Even if it wasn't the Crown Prince's directive but the Emperor's wish.

It was a natural disaster.

My father should have read the Emperor's mood earlier and pleased him, but he failed to do so.

"Still, I would like to escape."

He turned around and whispered something to his shadow, then tossed me a bottle containing some transparent liquid.

I caught it.

"You said you don't like pain.

If you drink that, you won't feel most pain."

Hearing that, I took a deep drag of smoke.

Is he giving me this because he wants me to die without any hesitation, or is he granting me death out of pity?

It doesn't really matter.

"Can you use magic to light tobacco, if not create flames?"

I nodded.

"Burn to death."

I imagined myself burning.

Only the shape of what was once human would remain, with no outward appearance left, which doesn't sound bad.

"I'm quite greedy, so I need to take not only your family but also the family attached to you."

"What can my burning to death accomplish?"

The Crown Prince pondered at my words.

Then he provided an answer.

"Not much will change.

But I can brand those honor-obsessed fools with the unthinkable stigma of having caused a girl they've lived with like family to burn to death.

Moral flaws are quite important for knights."

"Hmm, I like that."
